luke:24:14.csb: Together they were discussing everything that had taken place.

luke:24:15.niv: As they talked and discussed these things with each other, Jesus himself came up and walked along with them;
luke:24:15.csb: And while they were discussing and arguing, Jesus himself came near and began to walk along with them.

luke:24:16.niv: but they were kept from recognizing him.
luke:24:16.csb: But they were prevented from recognizing him.

luke:24:17.niv: He asked them, "What are you discussing together as you walk along?"
luke:24:17.csb: Then he asked them, “What is this dispute that you’re having with each other as you are walking?” And they stopped walking and looked discouraged.

luke:24:18.niv: They stood still, their faces downcast. One of them, named Cleopas, asked him, "Are you only a visitor to Jerusalem and do not know the things that have happened there in these days?"
luke:24:18.csb: The one named Cleopas answered him, “Are you the only visitor in Jerusalem who doesn’t know the things that happened there in these days?”

luke:24:19.csb: “What things?” he asked them. So they said to him, “The things concerning Jesus of Nazareth, who was a prophet powerful in action and speech before God and all the people,

luke:24:20.niv: "About Jesus of Nazareth," they replied. "He was a prophet, powerful in word and deed before God and all the people. The chief priests and our rulers handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him;
luke:24:20.csb: and how our chief priests and leaders handed him over to be sentenced to death, and they crucified him.

luke:24:21.niv: but we had hoped that he was the one who was going to redeem Israel. And what is more, it is the third day since all this took place.
luke:24:21.csb: But we were hoping that he was the one who was about to redeem Israel. Besides all this, it’s the third day since these things happened.

luke:24:22.niv: In addition, some of our women amazed us. They went to the tomb early this morning
luke:24:22.csb: Moreover, some women from our group astounded us. They arrived early at the tomb,

luke:24:23.niv: but didn't find his body. They came and told us that they had seen a vision of angels, who said he was alive.
luke:24:23.csb: and when they didn’t find his body, they came and reported that they had seen a vision of angels who said he was alive.

luke:24:24.niv: Then some of our companions went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but him they did not see."
luke:24:24.csb: Some of those who were with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said, but they didn’t see him.”

luke:24:25.niv: He said to them, "How foolish you are, and how slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken!
luke:24:25.csb: He said to them, “How foolish and slow you are to believe all that the prophets have spoken!

luke:24:26.niv: Did not the Christ have to suffer these things and then enter his glory?"
luke:24:26.csb: Wasn’t it necessary for the Messiah to suffer these things and enter into his glory?”

luke:24:27.niv: And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he explained to them what was said in all the Scriptures concerning himself.
luke:24:27.csb: Then beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he interpreted for them the things concerning himself in all the Scriptures.

luke:24:28.niv: As they approached the village to which they were going, Jesus acted as if he were going farther.
luke:24:28.csb: They came near the village where they were going, and he gave the impression that he was going farther.

luke:24:29.niv: But they urged him strongly, "Stay with us, for it is nearly evening; the day is almost over." So he went in to stay with them.
luke:24:29.csb: But they urged him, “Stay with us, because it’s almost evening, and now the day is almost over.” So he went in to stay with them.

luke:24:30.niv: When he was at the table with them, he took bread, gave thanks, broke it and began to give it to them.
luke:24:30.csb: It was as he reclined at the table with them that he took the bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them.

luke:24:31.niv: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized him, and he disappeared from their sight.
luke:24:31.csb: Then their eyes were opened, and they recognized him, but he disappeared from their sight.

luke:24:32.niv: They asked each other, "Were not our hearts burning within us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?"
luke:24:32.csb: They said to each other, “Weren’t our hearts burning within us while he was talking with us on the road and explaining the Scriptures to us?”

luke:24:33.niv: They got up and returned at once to Jerusalem. There they found the Eleven and those with them, assembled together
luke:24:33.csb: That very hour they got up and returned to Jerusalem. They found the Eleven and those with them gathered together,

luke:24:34.niv: and saying, "It is true! The Lord has risen and has appeared to Simon."
luke:24:34.csb: who said, “The Lord has truly been raised and has appeared to Simon!”

luke:24:35.niv: Then the two told what had happened on the way, and how Jesus was recognized by them when he broke the bread.
luke:24:35.csb: Then they began to describe what had happened on the road and how he was made known to them in the breaking of the bread.

luke:24:36.niv: While they were still talking about this, Jesus himself stood among them and said to them, "Peace be with you."
luke:24:36.csb: As they were saying these things, he himself stood in their midst. He said to them, “Peace to you!”

luke:24:37.niv: They were startled and frightened, thinking they saw a ghost.
luke:24:37.csb: But they were startled and terrified and thought they were seeing a ghost.

luke:24:38.niv: He said to them, "Why are you troubled, and why do doubts rise in your minds?
luke:24:38.csb: “Why are you troubled?” he asked them. “And why do doubts arise in your hearts?

luke:24:39.niv: Look at my hands and my feet. It is I myself! Touch me and see; a ghost does not have flesh and bones, as you see I have."
luke:24:39.csb: Look at my hands and my feet, that it is I myself! Touch me and see, because a ghost does not have flesh and bones as you can see I have.”

luke:24:40.niv: When he had said this, he showed them his hands and feet.
luke:24:40.csb: Having said this, he showed them his hands and feet.

luke:24:41.niv: And while they still did not believe it because of joy and amazement, he asked them, "Do you have anything here to eat?"
luke:24:41.csb: But while they still were amazed and in disbelief because of their joy, he asked them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”

luke:24:42.niv: They gave him a piece of broiled fish,
luke:24:42.csb: So they gave him a piece of a broiled fish,

luke:24:43.niv: and he took it and ate it in their presence.
luke:24:43.csb: and he took it and ate in their presence.

luke:24:44.niv: He said to them, "This is what I told you while I was still with you: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets and the Psalms."
luke:24:44.csb: He told them, “These are my words that I spoke to you while I was still with you — that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets, and the Psalms must be fulfilled.”

luke:24:45.niv: Then he opened their minds so they could understand the Scriptures.
luke:24:45.csb: Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures.

luke:24:46.niv: He told them, "This is what is written: The Christ will suffer and rise from the dead on the third day,
luke:24:46.csb: He also said to them, “This is what is written: The Messiah would suffer and rise from the dead the third day,

luke:24:47.niv: and repentance and forgiveness of sins will be preached in his name to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
luke:24:47.csb: and repentance for forgiveness of sins would be proclaimed in his name to all the nations, beginning at Jerusalem.

luke:24:48.niv: You are witnesses of these things.
luke:24:48.csb: You are witnesses of these things.

luke:24:49.niv: I am going to send you what my Father has promised; but stay in the city until you have been clothed with power from on high."
luke:24:49.csb: And look, I am sending you what my Father promised. As for you, stay in the city until you are empowered from on high.”

luke:24:50.niv: When he had led them out to the vicinity of Bethany, he lifted up his hands and blessed them.
luke:24:50.csb: Then he led them out to the vicinity of Bethany, and lifting up his hands he blessed them.

luke:24:51.niv: While he was blessing them, he left them and was taken up into heaven.
luke:24:51.csb: And while he was blessing them, he left them and was carried up into heaven.

luke:24:52.niv: Then they worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy.
luke:24:52.csb: After worshiping him, they returned to Jerusalem with great joy.

luke:24:53.niv: And they stayed continually at the temple, praising God.
luke:24:53.csb: And they were continually in the temple praising God.

john:1:1.niv: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
john:1:1.csb: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

john:1:2.niv: He was with God in the beginning.
john:1:2.csb: He was with God in the beginning.

john:1:3.niv: Through him all things were made; without him nothing was made that has been made.
john:1:3.csb: All things were created through him, and apart from him not one thing was created that has been created.

john:1:4.niv: In him was life, and that life was the light of men.
john:1:4.csb: In him was life, and that life was the light of men.

john:1:5.niv: The light shines in the darkness, but the darkness has not understood it.
john:1:5.csb: That light shines in the darkness, and yet the darkness did not overcome it.

john:1:6.niv: There came a man who was sent from God; his name was John.
john:1:6.csb: There was a man sent from God whose name was John.

john:1:7.niv: He came as a witness to testify concerning that light, so that through him all men might believe.
john:1:7.csb: He came as a witness to testify about the light, so that all might believe through him.

john:1:8.niv: He himself was not the light; he came only as a witness to the light.
john:1:8.csb: He was not the light, but he came to testify about the light.

john:1:9.niv: The true light that gives light to every man was coming into the world.
john:1:9.csb: The true light that gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.

john:1:10.niv: He was in the world, and though the world was made through him, the world did not recognize him.
john:1:10.csb: He was in the world, and the world was created through him, and yet the world did not recognize him.

john:1:11.niv: He came to that which was his own, but his own did not receive him.
john:1:11.csb: He came to his own, and his own people did not receive him.

john:1:12.niv: Yet to all who received him, to those who believed in his name, he gave the right to become children of God--
john:1:12.csb: But to all who did receive him, he gave them the right to be children of God, to those who believe in his name,

john:1:13.niv: children born not of natural descent, nor of human decision or a husband's will, but born of God.
john:1:13.csb: who were born, not of natural descent, or of the will of the flesh, or of the will of man, but of God.

john:1:14.niv: The Word became flesh and made his dwelling among us. We have seen his glory, the glory of the One and Only, who came from the Father, full of grace and truth.
john:1:14.csb: The Word became flesh and dwelt among us. We observed his glory, the glory as the one and only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth.

john:1:15.niv: John testifies concerning him. He cries out, saying, "This was he of whom I said, 'He who comes after me has surpassed me because he was before me.'"
john:1:15.csb: (John testified concerning him and exclaimed, “This was the one of whom I said, ‘The one coming after me ranks ahead of me, because he existed before me.’”)

john:1:16.niv: From the fullness of his grace we have all received one blessing after another.
john:1:16.csb: Indeed, we have all received grace upon grace from his fullness,

john:1:17.niv: For the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
john:1:17.csb: for the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.

john:1:18.niv: No one has ever seen God, but God the One and Only,, who is at the Father's side, has made him known.
john:1:18.csb: No one has ever seen God. The one and only Son, who is himself God and is at the Father’s side —he has revealed him.

john:1:19.niv: Now this was John's testimony when the Jews of Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him who he was.
john:1:19.csb: This was John’s testimony when the Jews from Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, “Who are you?”

john:1:20.niv: He did not fail to confess, but confessed freely, "I am not the Christ. "
john:1:20.csb: He didn’t deny it but confessed: “I am not the Messiah.”

john:1:21.niv: They asked him, "Then who are you? Are you Elijah?" He said, "I am not.Are you the Prophet?" He answered, "No."
john:1:21.csb: “What then?” they asked him. “Are you Elijah?” “I am not,” he said. “Are you the Prophet?” “No,” he answered.

john:1:22.niv: Finally they said, "Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?"
john:1:22.csb: “Who are you, then?” they asked. “We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What can you tell us about yourself?”

john:1:23.niv: John replied in the words of Isaiah the prophet, "I am the voice of one calling in the desert, 'Make straight the way for the Lord.'"
john:1:23.csb: He said, “I am a voice of one crying out in the wilderness: Make straight the way of the Lord — just as Isaiah the prophet said.”

john:1:24.niv: Now some Pharisees who had been sent
john:1:24.csb: Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.

john:1:25.niv: questioned him, "Why then do you baptize if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?"
john:1:25.csb: So they asked him, “Why then do you baptize if you aren’t the Messiah, or Elijah, or the Prophet?”

john:1:26.niv: "I baptize with water," John replied, "but among you stands one you do not know.
john:1:26.csb: “I baptize with water,” John answered them. “Someone stands among you, but you don’t know him.

john:1:27.niv: He is the one who comes after me, the thongs of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie."
john:1:27.csb: He is the one coming after me, whose sandal strap I’m not worthy to untie.”

john:1:28.niv: This all happened at Bethany on the other side of the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
john:1:28.csb: All this happened in Bethany across the Jordan, where John was baptizing.

john:1:29.niv: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, "Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
john:1:29.csb: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Here is the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!

john:1:30.niv: This is the one I meant when I said, 'A man who comes after me has surpassed me because he was before me.'
john:1:30.csb: This is the one I told you about: ‘After me comes a man who ranks ahead of me, because he existed before me.’

john:1:31.niv: I myself did not know him, but the reason I came baptizing with water was that he might be revealed to Israel."
john:1:31.csb: I didn’t know him, but I came baptizing with water so he might be revealed to Israel.”

john:1:32.niv: Then John gave this testimony: "I saw the Spirit come down from heaven as a dove and remain on him.
john:1:32.csb: And John testified, “I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and he rested on him.

john:1:33.niv: I would not have known him, except that the one who sent me to baptize with water told me, 'The man on whom you see the Spirit come down and remain is he who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.'
john:1:33.csb: I didn’t know him, but he who sent me to baptize with water told me, ‘The one you see the Spirit descending and resting on — he is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’

john:1:34.niv: I have seen and I testify that this is the Son of God."
john:1:34.csb: I have seen and testified that this is the Son of God.”

john:1:35.niv: The next day John was there again with two of his disciples.
john:1:35.csb: The next day, John was standing with two of his disciples.

john:1:36.niv: When he saw Jesus passing by, he said, "Look, the Lamb of God!"
john:1:36.csb: When he saw Jesus passing by, he said, “Look, the Lamb of God!”

john:1:37.niv: When the two disciples heard him say this, they followed Jesus.
john:1:37.csb: The two disciples heard him say this and followed Jesus.

john:1:38.niv: Turning around, Jesus saw them following and asked, "What do you want?" They said, "Rabbi" (which means Teacher), "where are you staying?"
john:1:38.csb: When Jesus turned and noticed them following him, he asked them, “What are you looking for?” They said to him, “Rabbi” (which means “Teacher”), “where are you staying?”

john:1:39.niv: "Come," he replied, "and you will see." So they went and saw where he was staying, and spent that day with him. It was about the tenth hour.
john:1:39.csb: “Come and you’ll see,” he replied. So they went and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about four in the afternoon.

john:1:40.niv: Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, was one of the two who heard what John had said and who had followed Jesus.
john:1:40.csb: Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of the two who heard John and followed him.

john:1:41.niv: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother Simon and tell him, "We have found the Messiah" (that is, the Christ).
john:1:41.csb: He first found his own brother Simon and told him, “We have found the Messiah” (which is translated “the Christ”),

john:1:42.niv: And he brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, "You are Simon son of John. You will be called Cephas" (which, when translated, is Peter ).
john:1:42.csb: and he brought Simon to Jesus. When Jesus saw him, he said, “You are Simon, son of John. You will be called Cephas” (which is translated “Peter” ).

john:1:43.niv: The next day Jesus decided to leave for Galilee. Finding Philip, he said to him, "Follow me."
john:1:43.csb: The next day Jesus decided to leave for Galilee. He found Philip and told him, “Follow me.”

john:1:44.niv: Philip, like Andrew and Peter, was from the town of Bethsaida.
john:1:44.csb: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the hometown of Andrew and Peter.

john:1:45.niv: Philip found Nathanael and told him, "We have found the one Moses wrote about in the Law, and about whom the prophets also wrote--Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph."
john:1:45.csb: Philip found Nathanael and told him, “We have found the one Moses wrote about in the law (and so did the prophets ): Jesus the son of Joseph, from Nazareth.”

john:1:46.niv: "Nazareth! Can anything good come from there?" Nathanael asked. "Come and see," said Philip.
john:1:46.csb: “Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Nathanael asked him. “Come and see,” Philip answered.

john:1:47.niv: When Jesus saw Nathanael approaching, he said of him, "Here is a true Israelite, in whom there is nothing false."
john:1:47.csb: Then Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said about him, “Here truly is an Israelite in whom there is no deceit.”

john:1:48.niv: "How do you know me?" Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, "I saw you while you were still under the fig tree before Philip called you."
john:1:48.csb: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you,” Jesus answered.

john:1:49.niv: Then Nathanael declared, "Rabbi, you are the Son of God; you are the King of Israel."
john:1:49.csb: “Rabbi,” Nathanael replied, “You are the Son of God; you are the King of Israel!”

john:1:50.niv: Jesus said, "You believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree. You shall see greater things than that."
john:1:50.csb: Jesus responded to him, “Do you believe because I told you I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than this.”

john:1:51.niv: He then added, "I tell you the truth, you shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man."
john:1:51.csb: Then he said, “Truly I tell you, you will see heaven opened and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”

john:2:1.niv: On the third day a wedding took place at Cana in Galilee. Jesus' mother was there,
john:2:1.csb: On the third day a wedding took place in Cana of Galilee. Jesus’s mother was there, and

john:2:2.niv: and Jesus and his disciples had also been invited to the wedding.
john:2:2.csb: Jesus and his disciples were invited to the wedding as well.

john:2:3.niv: When the wine was gone, Jesus' mother said to him, "They have no more wine."
john:2:3.csb: When the wine ran out, Jesus’s mother told him, “They don’t have any wine.”

john:2:4.niv: "Dear woman, why do you involve me?" Jesus replied, "My time has not yet come."
john:2:4.csb: “What does that have to do with you and me, woman?” Jesus asked. “My hour has not yet come.”

john:2:5.niv: His mother said to the servants, "Do whatever he tells you."
john:2:5.csb: “Do whatever he tells you,” his mother told the servants.

john:2:6.niv: Nearby stood six stone water jars, the kind used by the Jews for ceremonial washing, each holding from twenty to thirty gallons.
john:2:6.csb: Now six stone water jars had been set there for Jewish purification. Each contained twenty or thirty gallons.

john:2:7.niv: Jesus said to the servants, "Fill the jars with water"; so they filled them to the brim.
john:2:7.csb: “Fill the jars with water,” Jesus told them. So they filled them to the brim.

john:2:8.niv: Then he told them, "Now draw some out and take it to the master of the banquet."
john:2:8.csb: Then he said to them, “Now draw some out and take it to the headwaiter.” And they did.

john:2:9.niv: They did so, and the master of the banquet tasted the water that had been turned into wine. He did not realize where it had come from, though the servants who had drawn the water knew. Then he called the bridegroom aside
john:2:9.csb: When the headwaiter tasted the water (after it had become wine), he did not know where it came from — though the servants who had drawn the water knew. He called the groom

john:2:10.niv: and said, "Everyone brings out the choice wine first and then the cheaper wine after the guests have had too much to drink; but you have saved the best till now."
john:2:10.csb: and told him, “Everyone sets out the fine wine first, then, after people are drunk, the inferior. But you have kept the fine wine until now.”

john:2:11.niv: This, the first of his miraculous signs, Jesus performed in Cana of Galilee. He thus revealed his glory, and his disciples put their faith in him.
john:2:11.csb: Jesus did this, the first of his signs, in Cana of Galilee. He revealed his glory, and his disciples believed in him.

john:2:12.niv: After this he went down to Capernaum with his mother and brothers and his disciples. There they stayed for a few days.
john:2:12.csb: After this, he went down to Capernaum, together with his mother, his brothers, and his disciples, and they stayed there only a few days.

john:2:13.niv: When it was almost time for the Jewish Passover, Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
john:2:13.csb: The Jewish Passover was near, and so Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

john:2:14.niv: In the temple courts he found men selling cattle, sheep and doves, and others sitting at tables exchanging money.
john:2:14.csb: In the temple he found people selling oxen, sheep, and doves, and he also found the money changers sitting there.

john:2:15.niv: So he made a whip out of cords, and drove all from the temple area, both sheep and cattle; he scattered the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.
john:2:15.csb: After making a whip out of cords, he drove everyone out of the temple with their sheep and oxen. He also poured out the money changers’ coins and overturned the tables.

john:2:16.niv: To those who sold doves he said, "Get these out of here! How dare you turn my Father's house into a market!"
john:2:16.csb: He told those who were selling doves, “Get these things out of here! Stop turning my Father’s house into a marketplace!”

john:2:17.niv: His disciples remembered that it is written: "Zeal for your house will consume me."
john:2:17.csb: And his disciples remembered that it is written: Zeal for your house will consume me.

john:2:18.niv: Then the Jews demanded of him, "What miraculous sign can you show us to prove your authority to do all this?"
john:2:18.csb: So the Jews replied to him, “What sign will you show us for doing these things?”

john:2:19.niv: Jesus answered them, "Destroy this temple, and I will raise it again in three days."
john:2:19.csb: Jesus answered, “Destroy this temple, and I will raise it up in three days.”

john:2:20.niv: The Jews replied, "It has taken forty-six years to build this temple, and you are going to raise it in three days?"
john:2:20.csb: Therefore the Jews said, “This temple took forty-six years to build, and will you raise it up in three days?”

john:2:21.niv: But the temple he had spoken of was his body.
john:2:21.csb: But he was speaking about the temple of his body.

john:2:22.niv: After he was raised from the dead, his disciples recalled what he had said. Then they believed the Scripture and the words that Jesus had spoken.
john:2:22.csb: So when he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this, and they believed the Scripture and the statement Jesus had made.

john:2:23.niv: Now while he was in Jerusalem at the Passover Feast, many people saw the miraculous signs he was doing and believed in his name.
john:2:23.csb: While he was in Jerusalem during the Passover Festival, many believed in his name when they saw the signs he was doing.

john:2:24.niv: But Jesus would not entrust himself to them, for he knew all men.
john:2:24.csb: Jesus, however, would not entrust himself to them, since he knew them all

john:2:25.niv: He did not need man's testimony about man, for he knew what was in a man.
john:2:25.csb: and because he did not need anyone to testify about man; for he himself knew what was in man.

john:3:1.niv: Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a member of the Jewish ruling council.
john:3:1.csb: There was a man from the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews.

john:3:2.niv: He came to Jesus at night and said, "Rabbi, we know you are a teacher who has come from God. For no one could perform the miraculous signs you are doing if God were not with him."
john:3:2.csb: This man came to him at night and said, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher who has come from God, for no one could perform these signs you do unless God were with him.”

john:3:3.niv: In reply Jesus declared, "I tell you the truth, no one can see the kingdom of God unless he is born again. "
john:3:3.csb: Jesus replied, “Truly I tell you, unless someone is born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.”

john:3:4.niv: "How can a man be born when he is old?" Nicodemus asked. "Surely he cannot enter a second time into his mother's womb to be born!"
john:3:4.csb: “How can anyone be born when he is old?” Nicodemus asked him. “Can he enter his mother’s womb a second time and be born?”

john:3:5.niv: Jesus answered, "I tell you the truth, no one can enter the kingdom of God unless he is born of water and the Spirit.
john:3:5.csb: Jesus answered, “Truly I tell you, unless someone is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.

john:3:6.niv: Flesh gives birth to flesh, but the Spirit gives birth to spirit.
john:3:6.csb: Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh, and whatever is born of the Spirit is spirit.

john:3:7.niv: You should not be surprised at my saying, 'You must be born again.'
john:3:7.csb: Do not be amazed that I told you that you must be born again.

john:3:8.niv: The wind blows wherever it pleases. You hear its sound, but you cannot tell where it comes from or where it is going. So it is with everyone born of the Spirit."
john:3:8.csb: The wind blows where it pleases, and you hear its sound, but you don’t know where it comes from or where it is going. So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.”

john:3:9.csb: “How can these things be?” asked Nicodemus.

john:3:10.niv: "You are Israel's teacher," said Jesus, "and do you not understand these things?
john:3:10.csb: “Are you a teacher of Israel and don’t know these things?” Jesus replied.

john:3:11.niv: I tell you the truth, we speak of what we know, and we testify to what we have seen, but still you people do not accept our testimony.
john:3:11.csb: “Truly I tell you, we speak what we know and we testify to what we have seen, but you do not accept our testimony.

john:3:12.niv: I have spoken to you of earthly things and you do not believe; how then will you believe if I speak of heavenly things?
john:3:12.csb: If I have told you about earthly things and you don’t believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?

john:3:13.niv: No one has ever gone into heaven except the one who came from heaven--the Son of Man.
john:3:13.csb: No one has ascended into heaven except the one who descended from heaven — the Son of Man.

john:3:14.niv: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the desert, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,
john:3:14.csb: “Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,

john:3:15.niv: that everyone who believes in him may have eternal life.
john:3:15.csb: so that everyone who believes in him may have eternal life.

john:3:16.niv: "For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.
john:3:16.csb: For God loved the world in this way: He gave his one and only Son, so that everyone who believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.

john:3:17.niv: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.
john:3:17.csb: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.

john:3:18.niv: Whoever believes in him is not condemned, but whoever does not believe stands condemned already because he has not believed in the name of God's one and only Son.
john:3:18.csb: Anyone who believes in him is not condemned, but anyone who does not believe is already condemned, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.

john:3:19.niv: This is the verdict: Light has come into the world, but men loved darkness instead of light because their deeds were evil.
john:3:19.csb: This is the judgment: The light has come into the world, and people loved darkness rather than the light because their deeds were evil.

john:3:20.niv: Everyone who does evil hates the light, and will not come into the light for fear that his deeds will be exposed.
john:3:20.csb: For everyone who does evil hates the light and avoids it, so that his deeds may not be exposed.

john:3:21.niv: But whoever lives by the truth comes into the light, so that it may be seen plainly that what he has done has been done through God."
john:3:21.csb: But anyone who lives by the truth comes to the light, so that his works may be shown to be accomplished by God.”

john:3:22.niv: After this, Jesus and his disciples went out into the Judean countryside, where he spent some time with them, and baptized.
john:3:22.csb: After this, Jesus and his disciples went to the Judean countryside, where he spent time with them and baptized.

john:3:23.niv: Now John also was baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water, and people were constantly coming to be baptized.
john:3:23.csb: John also was baptizing in Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water there. People were coming and being baptized,

john:3:24.niv: (This was before John was put in prison.)
john:3:24.csb: since John had not yet been thrown into prison.

john:3:25.niv: An argument developed between some of John's disciples and a certain Jew over the matter of ceremonial washing.
john:3:25.csb: Then a dispute arose between John’s disciples and a Jew about purification.

john:3:26.niv: They came to John and said to him, "Rabbi, that man who was with you on the other side of the Jordan--the one you testified about--well, he is baptizing, and everyone is going to him."
john:3:26.csb: So they came to John and told him, “Rabbi, the one you testified about, and who was with you across the Jordan, is baptizing — and everyone is going to him.”

john:3:27.niv: To this John replied, "A man can receive only what is given him from heaven.
john:3:27.csb: John responded, “No one can receive anything unless it has been given to him from heaven.

john:3:28.niv: You yourselves can testify that I said, 'I am not the Christ but am sent ahead of him.'
john:3:28.csb: You yourselves can testify that I said, ‘I am not the Messiah, but I’ve been sent ahead of him.’

john:3:29.niv: The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend who attends the bridegroom waits and listens for him, and is full of joy when he hears the bridegroom's voice. That joy is mine, and it is now complete.
john:3:29.csb: He who has the bride is the groom. But the groom’s friend, who stands by and listens for him, rejoices greatly at the groom’s voice. So this joy of mine is complete.

john:3:30.niv: He must become greater; I must become less.
john:3:30.csb: He must increase, but I must decrease.”

john:3:31.niv: "The one who comes from above is above all; the one who is from the earth belongs to the earth, and speaks as one from the earth. The one who comes from heaven is above all.
john:3:31.csb: The one who comes from above is above all. The one who is from the earth is earthly and speaks in earthly terms. The one who comes from heaven is above all.

john:3:32.niv: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, but no one accepts his testimony.
john:3:32.csb: He testifies to what he has seen and heard, and yet no one accepts his testimony.

john:3:33.niv: The man who has accepted it has certified that God is truthful.
john:3:33.csb: The one who has accepted his testimony has affirmed that God is true.

john:3:34.niv: For the one whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for God gives the Spirit without limit.
john:3:34.csb: For the one whom God sent speaks God’s words, since he gives the Spirit without measure.

john:3:35.niv: The Father loves the Son and has placed everything in his hands.
john:3:35.csb: The Father loves the Son and has given all things into his hands.

john:3:36.niv: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life, but whoever rejects the Son will not see life, for God's wrath remains on him."
john:3:36.csb: The one who believes in the Son has eternal life, but the one who rejects the Son will not see life; instead, the wrath of God remains on him.

john:4:1.niv: The Pharisees heard that Jesus was gaining and baptizing more disciples than John,
john:4:1.csb: When Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard he was making and baptizing more disciples than John

john:4:2.niv: although in fact it was not Jesus who baptized, but his disciples.
john:4:2.csb: (though Jesus himself was not baptizing, but his disciples were),

john:4:3.niv: When the Lord learned of this, he left Judea and went back once more to Galilee.
john:4:3.csb: he left Judea and went again to Galilee.

john:4:4.niv: Now he had to go through Samaria.
john:4:4.csb: He had to travel through Samaria;

john:4:5.niv: So he came to a town in Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of ground Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
john:4:5.csb: so he came to a town of Samaria called Sychar near the property that Jacob had given his son Joseph.

john:4:6.niv: Jacob's well was there, and Jesus, tired as he was from the journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour.
john:4:6.csb: Jacob’s well was there, and Jesus, worn out from his journey, sat down at the well. It was about noon.

john:4:7.niv: When a Samaritan woman came to draw water, Jesus said to her, "Will you give me a drink?"
john:4:7.csb: A woman of Samaria came to draw water. “Give me a drink,” Jesus said to her,

john:4:8.niv: (His disciples had gone into the town to buy food.)
john:4:8.csb: because his disciples had gone into town to buy food.

john:4:9.niv: The Samaritan woman said to him, "You are a Jew and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink?" (For Jews do not associate with Samaritans. )
john:4:9.csb: “How is it that you, a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” she asked him. For Jews do not associate with Samaritans.

john:4:10.niv: Jesus answered her, "If you knew the gift of God and who it is that asks you for a drink, you would have asked him and he would have given you living water."
john:4:10.csb: Jesus answered, “If you knew the gift of God, and who is saying to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would ask him, and he would give you living water.”

john:4:11.niv: "Sir," the woman said, "you have nothing to draw with and the well is deep. Where can you get this living water?
john:4:11.csb: “Sir,” said the woman, “you don’t even have a bucket, and the well is deep. So where do you get this ‘living water’?

john:4:12.niv: Are you greater than our father Jacob, who gave us the well and drank from it himself, as did also his sons and his flocks and herds?"
john:4:12.csb: You aren’t greater than our father Jacob, are you? He gave us the well and drank from it himself, as did his sons and livestock.”

john:4:13.niv: Jesus answered, "Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again,
john:4:13.csb: Jesus said, “Everyone who drinks from this water will get thirsty again.

john:4:14.niv: but whoever drinks the water I give him will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give him will become in him a spring of water welling up to eternal life."
john:4:14.csb: But whoever drinks from the water that I will give him will never get thirsty again. In fact, the water I will give him will become a well of water springing up in him for eternal life.”

john:4:15.niv: The woman said to him, "Sir, give me this water so that I won't get thirsty and have to keep coming here to draw water."
john:4:15.csb: “Sir,” the woman said to him, “give me this water so that I won’t get thirsty and come here to draw water.”

john:4:16.niv: He told her, "Go, call your husband and come back."
john:4:16.csb: “Go call your husband,” he told her, “and come back here.”

john:4:17.csb: “I don’t have a husband,” she answered. “You have correctly said, ‘I don’t have a husband,’” Jesus said.

john:4:18.niv: Jesus said to her, "You are right when you say you have no husband. The fact is, you have had five husbands, and the man you now have is not your husband. What you have just said is quite true."
john:4:18.csb: “For you’ve had five husbands, and the man you now have is not your husband. What you have said is true.”

john:4:19.niv: "Sir," the woman said, "I can see that you are a prophet.
john:4:19.csb: “Sir,” the woman replied, “I see that you are a prophet.

john:4:20.niv: Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews claim that the place where we must worship is in Jerusalem."
john:4:20.csb: Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say that the place to worship is in Jerusalem.”

john:4:21.niv: Jesus declared, "Believe me, woman, a time is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
john:4:21.csb: Jesus told her, “Believe me, woman, an hour is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.

john:4:22.niv: You Samaritans worship what you do not know; we worship what we do know, for salvation is from the Jews.
john:4:22.csb: You Samaritans worship what you do not know. We worship what we do know, because salvation is from the Jews.

john:4:23.niv: Yet a time is coming and has now come when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for they are the kind of worshipers the Father seeks.
john:4:23.csb: But an hour is coming, and is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in Spirit and in truth. Yes, the Father wants such people to worship him.

john:4:24.niv: God is spirit, and his worshipers must worship in spirit and in truth."
john:4:24.csb: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in Spirit and in truth.”

john:4:25.niv: The woman said, "I know that Messiah" (called Christ) "is coming. When he comes, he will explain everything to us."
john:4:25.csb: The woman said to him, “I know that the Messiah is coming” (who is called Christ ). “When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”

john:4:26.niv: Then Jesus declared, "I who speak to you am he."
john:4:26.csb: Jesus told her, “I, the one speaking to you, am he.”

john:4:27.niv: Just then his disciples returned and were surprised to find him talking with a woman. But no one asked, "What do you want?" or "Why are you talking with her?"
john:4:27.csb: Just then his disciples arrived, and they were amazed that he was talking with a woman. Yet no one said, “What do you want?” or “Why are you talking with her?”

john:4:28.niv: Then, leaving her water jar, the woman went back to the town and said to the people,
john:4:28.csb: Then the woman left her water jar, went into town, and told the people,

john:4:29.niv: "Come, see a man who told me everything I ever did. Could this be the Christ?"
john:4:29.csb: “Come, see a man who told me everything I ever did. Could this be the Messiah?”

john:4:30.niv: They came out of the town and made their way toward him.
john:4:30.csb: They left the town and made their way to him.

john:4:31.niv: Meanwhile his disciples urged him, "Rabbi, eat something."
john:4:31.csb: In the meantime the disciples kept urging him, “Rabbi, eat something.”

john:4:32.niv: But he said to them, "I have food to eat that you know nothing about."
john:4:32.csb: But he said, “I have food to eat that you don’t know about.”

john:4:33.niv: Then his disciples said to each other, "Could someone have brought him food?"
john:4:33.csb: The disciples said to one another, “Could someone have brought him something to eat?”

john:4:34.niv: "My food," said Jesus, "is to do the will of him who sent me and to finish his work.
john:4:34.csb: “My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to finish his work,” Jesus told them.

john:4:35.niv: Do you not say, 'Four months more and then the harvest'? I tell you, open your eyes and look at the fields! They are ripe for harvest.
john:4:35.csb: “Don’t you say, ‘There are still four more months, and then comes the harvest’? Listen to what I’m telling you: Open your eyes and look at the fields, because they are ready for harvest.

john:4:36.niv: Even now the reaper draws his wages, even now he harvests the crop for eternal life, so that the sower and the reaper may be glad together.
john:4:36.csb: The reaper is already receiving pay and gathering fruit for eternal life, so that the sower and reaper can rejoice together.

john:4:37.niv: Thus the saying 'One sows and another reaps' is true.
john:4:37.csb: For in this case the saying is true: ‘One sows and another reaps.’

john:4:38.niv: I sent you to reap what you have not worked for. Others have done the hard work, and you have reaped the benefits of their labor."
john:4:38.csb: I sent you to reap what you didn’t labor for; others have labored, and you have benefited from their labor.”

john:4:39.niv: Many of the Samaritans from that town believed in him because of the woman's testimony, "He told me everything I ever did."
john:4:39.csb: Now many Samaritans from that town believed in him because of what the woman said when she testified, “He told me everything I ever did.”

john:4:40.niv: So when the Samaritans came to him, they urged him to stay with them, and he stayed two days.
john:4:40.csb: So when the Samaritans came to him, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.

john:4:41.niv: And because of his words many more became believers.
john:4:41.csb: Many more believed because of what he said.

john:4:42.niv: They said to the woman, "We no longer believe just because of what you said; now we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this man really is the Savior of the world."
john:4:42.csb: And they told the woman, “We no longer believe because of what you said, since we have heard for ourselves and know that this really is the Savior of the world.”

john:4:43.niv: After the two days he left for Galilee.
john:4:43.csb: After two days he left there for Galilee.

john:4:44.niv: (Now Jesus himself had pointed out that a prophet has no honor in his own country.)
john:4:44.csb: (Jesus himself had testified that a prophet has no honor in his own country. )

john:4:45.niv: When he arrived in Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him. They had seen all that he had done in Jerusalem at the Passover Feast, for they also had been there.
john:4:45.csb: When they entered Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him because they had seen everything he did in Jerusalem during the festival. For they also had gone to the festival.

john:4:46.niv: Once more he visited Cana in Galilee, where he had turned the water into wine. And there was a certain royal official whose son lay sick at Capernaum.
john:4:46.csb: He went again to Cana of Galilee, where he had turned the water into wine. There was a certain royal official whose son was ill at Capernaum.

john:4:47.niv: When this man heard that Jesus had arrived in Galilee from Judea, he went to him and begged him to come and heal his son, who was close to death.
john:4:47.csb: When this man heard that Jesus had come from Judea into Galilee, he went to him and pleaded with him to come down and heal his son, since he was about to die.

john:4:48.niv: "Unless you people see miraculous signs and wonders," Jesus told him, "you will never believe."
john:4:48.csb: Jesus told him, “Unless you people see signs and wonders, you will not believe.”

john:4:49.niv: The royal official said, "Sir, come down before my child dies."
john:4:49.csb: “Sir,” the official said to him, “come down before my boy dies.”

john:4:50.niv: Jesus replied, "You may go. Your son will live." The man took Jesus at his word and departed.
john:4:50.csb: “Go,” Jesus told him, “your son will live.” The man believed what Jesus said to him and departed.

john:4:51.niv: While he was still on the way, his servants met him with the news that his boy was living.
john:4:51.csb: While he was still going down, his servants met him saying that his boy was alive.

john:4:52.niv: When he inquired as to the time when his son got better, they said to him, "The fever left him yesterday at the seventh hour."
john:4:52.csb: He asked them at what time he got better. “Yesterday at one in the afternoon the fever left him,” they answered.

john:4:53.niv: Then the father realized that this was the exact time at which Jesus had said to him, "Your son will live." So he and all his household believed.
john:4:53.csb: The father realized this was the very hour at which Jesus had told him, “Your son will live.” So he himself believed, along with his whole household.

john:4:54.niv: This was the second miraculous sign that Jesus performed, having come from Judea to Galilee.
john:4:54.csb: Now this was also the second sign Jesus performed after he came from Judea to Galilee.

john:5:1.niv: Some time later, Jesus went up to Jerusalem for a feast of the Jews.
john:5:1.csb: After this, a Jewish festival took place, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

john:5:2.niv: Now there is in Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate a pool, which in Aramaic is called Bethesda and which is surrounded by five covered colonnades.
john:5:2.csb: By the Sheep Gate in Jerusalem there is a pool, called Bethesda in Aramaic, which has five colonnades.

john:5:3.niv: Here a great number of disabled people used to lie--the blind, the lame, the paralyzed.
john:5:3.csb: Within these lay a large number of the disabled — blind, lame, and paralyzed.

john:5:5.niv: One who was there had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.
john:5:5.csb: One man was there who had been disabled for thirty-eight years.

john:5:6.niv: When Jesus saw him lying there and learned that he had been in this condition for a long time, he asked him, "Do you want to get well?"
john:5:6.csb: When Jesus saw him lying there and realized he had already been there a long time, he said to him, “Do you want to get well?”

john:5:7.niv: "Sir," the invalid replied, "I have no one to help me into the pool when the water is stirred. While I am trying to get in, someone else goes down ahead of me."
john:5:7.csb: “Sir,” the disabled man answered, “I have no one to put me into the pool when the water is stirred up, but while I’m coming, someone goes down ahead of me.”

john:5:8.niv: Then Jesus said to him, "Get up! Pick up your mat and walk."
john:5:8.csb: “Get up,” Jesus told him, “pick up your mat and walk.”

john:5:9.niv: At once the man was cured; he picked up his mat and walked. The day on which this took place was a Sabbath,
john:5:9.csb: Instantly the man got well, picked up his mat, and started to walk. Now that day was the Sabbath,

john:5:10.niv: and so the Jews said to the man who had been healed, "It is the Sabbath; the law forbids you to carry your mat."
john:5:10.csb: and so the Jews said to the man who had been healed, “This is the Sabbath. The law prohibits you from picking up your mat.”

john:5:11.niv: But he replied, "The man who made me well said to me, 'Pick up your mat and walk.'"
john:5:11.csb: He replied, “The man who made me well told me, ‘Pick up your mat and walk.’ ”

john:5:12.niv: So they asked him, "Who is this fellow who told you to pick it up and walk?"
john:5:12.csb: “Who is this man who told you, ‘Pick up your mat and walk’?” they asked.

john:5:13.niv: The man who was healed had no idea who it was, for Jesus had slipped away into the crowd that was there.
john:5:13.csb: But the man who was healed did not know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away into the crowd that was there.

john:5:14.niv: Later Jesus found him at the temple and said to him, "See, you are well again. Stop sinning or something worse may happen to you."
john:5:14.csb: After this, Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, “See, you are well. Do not sin anymore, so that something worse doesn’t happen to you.”

john:5:15.niv: The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
john:5:15.csb: The man went and reported to the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.

john:5:16.niv: So, because Jesus was doing these things on the Sabbath, the Jews persecuted him.
john:5:16.csb: Therefore, the Jews began persecuting Jesus because he was doing these things on the Sabbath.

john:5:17.niv: Jesus said to them, "My Father is always at his work to this very day, and I, too, am working."
john:5:17.csb: Jesus responded to them, “My Father is still working, and I am working also.”

john:5:18.niv: For this reason the Jews tried all the harder to kill him; not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was even calling God his own Father, making himself equal with God.
john:5:18.csb: This is why the Jews began trying all the more to kill him: Not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was even calling God his own Father, making himself equal to God.

john:5:19.niv: Jesus gave them this answer: "I tell you the truth, the Son can do nothing by himself; he can do only what he sees his Father doing, because whatever the Father does the Son also does.
john:5:19.csb: Jesus replied, “Truly I tell you, the Son is not able to do anything on his own, but only what he sees the Father doing. For whatever the Father does, the Son likewise does these things.

john:5:20.niv: For the Father loves the Son and shows him all he does. Yes, to your amazement he will show him even greater things than these.
john:5:20.csb: For the Father loves the Son and shows him everything he is doing, and he will show him greater works than these so that you will be amazed.

john:5:21.niv: For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, even so the Son gives life to whom he is pleased to give it.
john:5:21.csb: And just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, so the Son also gives life to whom he wants.

john:5:22.niv: Moreover, the Father judges no one, but has entrusted all judgment to the Son,
john:5:22.csb: The Father, in fact, judges no one but has given all judgment to the Son,

john:5:23.niv: that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. He who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father, who sent him.
john:5:23.csb: so that all people may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Anyone who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.

john:5:24.niv: "I tell you the truth, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life and will not be condemned; he has crossed over from death to life.
john:5:24.csb: “Truly I tell you, anyone who hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life and will not come under judgment but has passed from death to life.

john:5:25.niv: I tell you the truth, a time is coming and has now come when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God and those who hear will live.
john:5:25.csb: “Truly I tell you, an hour is coming, and is now here, when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.

john:5:26.niv: For as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself.
john:5:26.csb: For just as the Father has life in himself, so also he has granted to the Son to have life in himself.

john:5:27.niv: And he has given him authority to judge because he is the Son of Man.
john:5:27.csb: And he has granted him the right to pass judgment, because he is the Son of Man.

john:5:28.niv: "Do not be amazed at this, for a time is coming when all who are in their graves will hear his voice
john:5:28.csb: Do not be amazed at this, because a time is coming when all who are in the graves will hear his voice

john:5:29.niv: and come out--those who have done good will rise to live, and those who have done evil will rise to be condemned.
john:5:29.csb: and come out — those who have done good things, to the resurrection of life, but those who have done wicked things, to the resurrection of condemnation.

john:5:30.niv: By myself I can do nothing; I judge only as I hear, and my judgment is just, for I seek not to please myself but him who sent me.
john:5:30.csb: “I can do nothing on my own. I judge only as I hear, and my judgment is just, because I do not seek my own will, but the will of him who sent me.

john:5:31.niv: "If I testify about myself, my testimony is not valid.
john:5:31.csb: “If I testify about myself, my testimony is not true.

john:5:32.niv: There is another who testifies in my favor, and I know that his testimony about me is valid.
john:5:32.csb: There is another who testifies about me, and I know that the testimony he gives about me is true.

john:5:33.niv: "You have sent to John and he has testified to the truth.
john:5:33.csb: You sent messengers to John, and he testified to the truth.

john:5:34.niv: Not that I accept human testimony; but I mention it that you may be saved.
john:5:34.csb: I don’t receive human testimony, but I say these things so that you may be saved.

john:5:35.niv: John was a lamp that burned and gave light, and you chose for a time to enjoy his light.
john:5:35.csb: John was a burning and shining lamp, and you were willing to rejoice for a while in his light.

john:5:36.niv: "I have testimony weightier than that of John. For the very work that the Father has given me to finish, and which I am doing, testifies that the Father has sent me.
john:5:36.csb: “But I have a greater testimony than John’s because of the works that the Father has given me to accomplish. These very works I am doing testify about me that the Father has sent me.

john:5:37.niv: And the Father who sent me has himself testified concerning me. You have never heard his voice nor seen his form,
john:5:37.csb: The Father who sent me has himself testified about me. You have not heard his voice at any time, and you haven’t seen his form.

john:5:38.niv: nor does his word dwell in you, for you do not believe the one he sent.
john:5:38.csb: You don’t have his word residing in you, because you don’t believe the one he sent.

john:5:39.niv: You diligently study the Scriptures because you think that by them you possess eternal life. These are the Scriptures that testify about me,
john:5:39.csb: You pore over the Scriptures because you think you have eternal life in them, and yet they testify about me.

john:5:40.niv: yet you refuse to come to me to have life.
john:5:40.csb: But you are not willing to come to me so that you may have life.

john:5:41.niv: "I do not accept praise from men,
john:5:41.csb: “I do not accept glory from people,

john:5:42.niv: but I know you. I know that you do not have the love of God in your hearts.
john:5:42.csb: but I know you — that you have no love for God within you.

john:5:43.niv: I have come in my Father's name, and you do not accept me; but if someone else comes in his own name, you will accept him.
john:5:43.csb: I have come in my Father’s name, and yet you don’t accept me. If someone else comes in his own name, you will accept him.

john:5:44.niv: How can you believe if you accept praise from one another, yet make no effort to obtain the praise that comes from the only God?
john:5:44.csb: How can you believe, since you accept glory from one another but don’t seek the glory that comes from the only God?

john:5:45.niv: "But do not think I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, on whom your hopes are set.
john:5:45.csb: Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father. Your accuser is Moses, on whom you have set your hope.

john:5:46.niv: If you believed Moses, you would believe me, for he wrote about me.
john:5:46.csb: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me, because he wrote about me.

john:5:47.niv: But since you do not believe what he wrote, how are you going to believe what I say?"
john:5:47.csb: But if you don’t believe what he wrote, how will you believe my words?”

john:6:1.niv: Some time after this, Jesus crossed to the far shore of the Sea of Galilee (that is, the Sea of Tiberias),
john:6:1.csb: After this, Jesus crossed the Sea of Galilee (or Tiberias ).

john:6:2.niv: and a great crowd of people followed him because they saw the miraculous signs he had performed on the sick.
john:6:2.csb: A huge crowd was following him because they saw the signs that he was performing by healing the sick.

john:6:3.niv: Then Jesus went up on a mountainside and sat down with his disciples.
john:6:3.csb: Jesus went up a mountain and sat down there with his disciples.

john:6:4.niv: The Jewish Passover Feast was near.
john:6:4.csb: Now the Passover, a Jewish festival, was near.

john:6:5.niv: When Jesus looked up and saw a great crowd coming toward him, he said to Philip, "Where shall we buy bread for these people to eat?"
john:6:5.csb: So when Jesus looked up and noticed a huge crowd coming toward him, he asked Philip, “Where will we buy bread so that these people can eat?”

john:6:6.niv: He asked this only to test him, for he already had in mind what he was going to do.
john:6:6.csb: He asked this to test him, for he himself knew what he was going to do.

john:6:7.niv: Philip answered him, "Eight months' wages would not buy enough bread for each one to have a bite!"
john:6:7.csb: Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread wouldn’t be enough for each of them to have a little.”

john:6:8.niv: Another of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, spoke up,
john:6:8.csb: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,

john:6:9.niv: "Here is a boy with five small barley loaves and two small fish, but how far will they go among so many?"
john:6:9.csb: “There’s a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish — but what are they for so many?”

john:6:10.niv: Jesus said, "Have the people sit down." There was plenty of grass in that place, and the men sat down, about five thousand of them.
john:6:10.csb: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” There was plenty of grass in that place; so they sat down. The men numbered about five thousand.

john:6:11.niv: Jesus then took the loaves, gave thanks, and distributed to those who were seated as much as they wanted. He did the same with the fish.
john:6:11.csb: Then Jesus took the loaves, and after giving thanks he distributed them to those who were seated — so also with the fish, as much as they wanted.

john:6:12.niv: When they had all had enough to eat, he said to his disciples, "Gather the pieces that are left over. Let nothing be wasted."
john:6:12.csb: When they were full, he told his disciples, “Collect the leftovers so that nothing is wasted.”

john:6:13.niv: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves left over by those who had eaten.
john:6:13.csb: So they collected them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces from the five barley loaves that were left over by those who had eaten.

john:6:14.niv: After the people saw the miraculous sign that Jesus did, they began to say, "Surely this is the Prophet who is to come into the world."
john:6:14.csb: When the people saw the sign he had done, they said, “This truly is the Prophet who is to come into the world.”

john:6:15.niv: Jesus, knowing that they intended to come and make him king by force, withdrew again to a mountain by himself.
john:6:15.csb: Therefore, when Jesus realized that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, he withdrew again to the mountain by himself.

john:6:16.niv: When evening came, his disciples went down to the lake,
john:6:16.csb: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,

john:6:17.niv: where they got into a boat and set off across the lake for Capernaum. By now it was dark, and Jesus had not yet joined them.
john:6:17.csb: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. Darkness had already set in, but Jesus had not yet come to them.

john:6:18.niv: A strong wind was blowing and the waters grew rough.
john:6:18.csb: A high wind arose, and the sea began to churn.

john:6:19.niv: When they had rowed three or three and a half miles, they saw Jesus approaching the boat, walking on the water; and they were terrified.
john:6:19.csb: After they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea. He was coming near the boat, and they were afraid.

john:6:20.niv: But he said to them, "It is I; don't be afraid."
john:6:20.csb: But he said to them, “It is I. Don’t be afraid.”

john:6:21.niv: Then they were willing to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat reached the shore where they were heading.
john:6:21.csb: Then they were willing to take him on board, and at once the boat was at the shore where they were heading.

john:6:22.niv: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the opposite shore of the lake realized that only one boat had been there, and that Jesus had not entered it with his disciples, but that they had gone away alone.
john:6:22.csb: The next day, the crowd that had stayed on the other side of the sea saw there had been only one boat. They also saw that Jesus had not boarded the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone off alone.

john:6:23.niv: Then some boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the people had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
john:6:23.csb: Some boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

john:6:24.niv: Once the crowd realized that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum in search of Jesus.
john:6:24.csb: When the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum looking for Jesus.

john:6:25.niv: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, "Rabbi, when did you get here?"
john:6:25.csb: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”

john:6:26.niv: Jesus answered, "I tell you the truth, you are looking for me, not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate the loaves and had your fill.
john:6:26.csb: Jesus answered, “Truly I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw the signs, but because you ate the loaves and were filled.

john:6:27.niv: Do not work for food that spoils, but for food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. On him God the Father has placed his seal of approval."
john:6:27.csb: Don’t work for the food that perishes but for the food that lasts for eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you, because God the Father has set his seal of approval on him.”

john:6:28.niv: Then they asked him, "What must we do to do the works God requires?"
john:6:28.csb: “What can we do to perform the works of God?” they asked.

john:6:29.niv: Jesus answered, "The work of God is this: to believe in the one he has sent."
john:6:29.csb: Jesus replied, “This is the work of God — that you believe in the one he has sent.”

john:6:30.niv: So they asked him, "What miraculous sign then will you give that we may see it and believe you? What will you do?
john:6:30.csb: “What sign, then, are you going to do so we may see and believe you?” they asked. “What are you going to perform?

john:6:31.niv: Our forefathers ate the manna in the desert; as it is written: 'He gave them bread from heaven to eat.'"
john:6:31.csb: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, just as it is written: He gave them bread from heaven to eat. ”

john:6:32.niv: Jesus said to them, "I tell you the truth, it is not Moses who has given you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.
john:6:32.csb: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, Moses didn’t give you the bread from heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.

john:6:33.niv: For the bread of God is he who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world."
john:6:33.csb: For the bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”

john:6:34.niv: "Sir," they said, "from now on give us this bread."
john:6:34.csb: Then they said, “Sir, give us this bread always.”

john:6:35.niv: Then Jesus declared, "I am the bread of life. He who comes to me will never go hungry, and he who believes in me will never be thirsty.
john:6:35.csb: “I am the bread of life,” Jesus told them. “No one who comes to me will ever be hungry, and no one who believes in me will ever be thirsty again.

john:6:36.niv: But as I told you, you have seen me and still you do not believe.
john:6:36.csb: But as I told you, you’ve seen me, and yet you do not believe.

john:6:37.niv: All that the Father gives me will come to me, and whoever comes to me I will never drive away.
john:6:37.csb: Everyone the Father gives me will come to me, and the one who comes to me I will never cast out.

john:6:38.niv: For I have come down from heaven not to do my will but to do the will of him who sent me.
john:6:38.csb: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.

john:6:39.niv: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I shall lose none of all that he has given me, but raise them up at the last day.
john:6:39.csb: This is the will of him who sent me: that I should lose none of those he has given me but should raise them up on the last day.

john:6:40.niv: For my Father's will is that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in him shall have eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day."
john:6:40.csb: For this is the will of my Father: that everyone who sees the Son and believes in him will have eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.”

john:6:41.niv: At this the Jews began to grumble about him because he said, "I am the bread that came down from heaven."
john:6:41.csb: Therefore the Jews started complaining about him because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”

john:6:42.niv: They said, "Is this not Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How can he now say, 'I came down from heaven'?"
john:6:42.csb: They were saying, “Isn’t this Jesus the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How can he now say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”

john:6:43.csb: Jesus answered them, “Stop complaining among yourselves.

john:6:44.niv: "No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up at the last day.
john:6:44.csb: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up on the last day.

john:6:45.niv: It is written in the Prophets: 'They will all be taught by God.' Everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him comes to me.
john:6:45.csb: It is written in the Prophets: And they will all be taught by God. Everyone who has listened to and learned from the Father comes to me —

john:6:46.niv: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God; only he has seen the Father.
john:6:46.csb: not that anyone has seen the Father except the one who is from God. He has seen the Father.

john:6:47.niv: I tell you the truth, he who believes has everlasting life.
john:6:47.csb: “Truly I tell you, anyone who believes has eternal life.

john:6:48.niv: I am the bread of life.
john:6:48.csb: I am the bread of life.

john:6:49.niv: Your forefathers ate the manna in the desert, yet they died.
john:6:49.csb: Your ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.

john:6:50.niv: But here is the bread that comes down from heaven, which a man may eat and not die.
john:6:50.csb: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that anyone may eat of it and not die.

john:6:51.niv: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. This bread is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world."
john:6:51.csb: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread he will live forever. The bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”

john:6:52.niv: Then the Jews began to argue sharply among themselves, "How can this man give us his flesh to eat?"
john:6:52.csb: At that, the Jews argued among themselves, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”

john:6:53.niv: Jesus said to them, "I tell you the truth, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
john:6:53.csb: So Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you do not have life in yourselves.

john:6:54.niv: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.
john:6:54.csb: The one who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day,

john:6:55.niv: For my flesh is real food and my blood is real drink.
john:6:55.csb: because my flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.

john:6:56.niv: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in him.
john:6:56.csb: The one who eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in him.

john:6:57.niv: Just as the living Father sent me and I live because of the Father, so the one who feeds on me will live because of me.
john:6:57.csb: Just as the living Father sent me and I live because of the Father, so the one who feeds on me will live because of me.

john:6:58.niv: This is the bread that came down from heaven. Your forefathers ate manna and died, but he who feeds on this bread will live forever."
john:6:58.csb: This is the bread that came down from heaven; it is not like the manna your ancestors ate — and they died. The one who eats this bread will live forever.”

john:6:59.niv: He said this while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
john:6:59.csb: He said these things while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.

john:6:60.niv: On hearing it, many of his disciples said, "This is a hard teaching. Who can accept it?"
john:6:60.csb: Therefore, when many of his disciples heard this, they said, “This teaching is hard. Who can accept it?”

john:6:61.niv: Aware that his disciples were grumbling about this, Jesus said to them, "Does this offend you?
john:6:61.csb: Jesus, knowing in himself that his disciples were complaining about this, asked them, “Does this offend you?

john:6:62.niv: What if you see the Son of Man ascend to where he was before!
john:6:62.csb: Then what if you were to observe the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?

john:6:63.niv: The Spirit gives life; the flesh counts for nothing. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and they are life.
john:6:63.csb: The Spirit is the one who gives life. The flesh doesn’t help at all. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and are life.

john:6:64.niv: Yet there are some of you who do not believe." For Jesus had known from the beginning which of them did not believe and who would betray him.
john:6:64.csb: But there are some among you who don’t believe.” (For Jesus knew from the beginning those who did not believe and the one who would betray him.)

john:6:65.niv: He went on to say, "This is why I told you that no one can come to me unless the Father has enabled him."
john:6:65.csb: He said, “This is why I told you that no one can come to me unless it is granted to him by the Father.”

john:6:66.niv: From this time many of his disciples turned back and no longer followed him.
john:6:66.csb: From that moment many of his disciples turned back and no longer accompanied him.

john:6:67.niv: "You do not want to leave too, do you?" Jesus asked the Twelve.
john:6:67.csb: So Jesus said to the Twelve, “You don’t want to go away too, do you?”

john:6:68.niv: Simon Peter answered him, "Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.
john:6:68.csb: Simon Peter answered, “Lord, to whom will we go? You have the words of eternal life.

john:6:69.niv: We believe and know that you are the Holy One of God."
john:6:69.csb: We have come to believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.”

john:6:70.niv: Then Jesus replied, "Have I not chosen you, the Twelve? Yet one of you is a devil!"
john:6:70.csb: Jesus replied to them, “Didn’t I choose you, the Twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.”

john:6:71.niv: (He meant Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, who, though one of the Twelve, was later to betray him.)
john:6:71.csb: He was referring to Judas, Simon Iscariot’s son, one of the Twelve, because he was going to betray him.

john:7:1.niv: After this, Jesus went around in Galilee, purposely staying away from Judea because the Jews there were waiting to take his life.
john:7:1.csb: After this, Jesus traveled in Galilee, since he did not want to travel in Judea because the Jews were trying to kill him.

john:7:2.niv: But when the Jewish Feast of Tabernacles was near,
john:7:2.csb: The Jewish Festival of Shelters was near.

john:7:3.niv: Jesus' brothers said to him, "You ought to leave here and go to Judea, so that your disciples may see the miracles you do.
john:7:3.csb: So his brothers said to him, “Leave here and go to Judea so your disciples can see your works that you are doing.

john:7:4.niv: No one who wants to become a public figure acts in secret. Since you are doing these things, show yourself to the world."
john:7:4.csb: For no one does anything in secret while he’s seeking public recognition. If you do these things, show yourself to the world.”

john:7:5.niv: For even his own brothers did not believe in him.
john:7:5.csb: (For not even his brothers believed in him.)

john:7:6.niv: Therefore Jesus told them, "The right time for me has not yet come; for you any time is right.
john:7:6.csb: Jesus told them, “My time has not yet arrived, but your time is always at hand.

john:7:7.niv: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me because I testify that what it does is evil.
john:7:7.csb: The world cannot hate you, but it does hate me because I testify about it — that its works are evil.

john:7:8.niv: You go to the Feast. I am not yet going up to this Feast, because for me the right time has not yet come."
john:7:8.csb: Go up to the festival yourselves. I’m not going up to this festival, because my time has not yet fully come.”

john:7:9.niv: Having said this, he stayed in Galilee.
john:7:9.csb: After he had said these things, he stayed in Galilee.

john:7:10.niv: However, after his brothers had left for the Feast, he went also, not publicly, but in secret.
john:7:10.csb: After his brothers had gone up to the festival, then he also went up, not openly but secretly.

john:7:11.niv: Now at the Feast the Jews were watching for him and asking, "Where is that man?"
john:7:11.csb: The Jews were looking for him at the festival and saying, “Where is he?”

john:7:12.niv: Among the crowds there was widespread whispering about him. Some said, "He is a good man."
john:7:12.csb: And there was a lot of murmuring about him among the crowds. Some were saying, “He’s a good man.” Others were saying, “No, on the contrary, he’s deceiving the people.”

john:7:13.niv: Others replied, "No, he deceives the people." But no one would say anything publicly about him for fear of the Jews.
john:7:13.csb: Still, nobody was talking publicly about him for fear of the Jews.

john:7:14.niv: Not until halfway through the Feast did Jesus go up to the temple courts and begin to teach.
john:7:14.csb: When the festival was already half over, Jesus went up into the temple and began to teach.

john:7:15.niv: The Jews were amazed and asked, "How did this man get such learning without having studied?"
john:7:15.csb: Then the Jews were amazed and said, “How is this man so learned, since he hasn’t been trained?”

john:7:16.niv: Jesus answered, "My teaching is not my own. It comes from him who sent me.
john:7:16.csb: Jesus answered them, “My teaching isn’t mine but is from the one who sent me.

john:7:17.niv: If anyone chooses to do God's will, he will find out whether my teaching comes from God or whether I speak on my own.
john:7:17.csb: If anyone wants to do his will, he will know whether the teaching is from God or whether I am speaking on my own.

john:7:18.niv: He who speaks on his own does so to gain honor for himself, but he who works for the honor of the one who sent him is a man of truth; there is nothing false about him.
john:7:18.csb: The one who speaks on his own seeks his own glory; but he who seeks the glory of the one who sent him is true, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

john:7:19.niv: Has not Moses given you the law? Yet not one of you keeps the law. Why are you trying to kill me?"
john:7:19.csb: Didn’t Moses give you the law? Yet none of you keeps the law. Why are you trying to kill me?”

john:7:20.niv: "You are demon-possessed," the crowd answered. "Who is trying to kill you?"
john:7:20.csb: “You have a demon!” the crowd responded. “Who is trying to kill you?”

john:7:21.niv: Jesus said to them, "I did one miracle, and you are all astonished.
john:7:21.csb: “I performed one work, and you are all amazed,” Jesus answered.

john:7:22.niv: Yet, because Moses gave you circumcision (though actually it did not come from Moses, but from the patriarchs), you circumcise a child on the Sabbath.
